headings.title: If you use the desktop app

pages.title: Installation
pages.description: Niamoto gives you two entry points: a desktop app and a Python CLI. Install the desktop app unless you need automation, CI, or server runs.

pages.title: Your first Niamoto project
pages.description: Follow the desktop app from the welcome screen to a published portal.

pages.title: Niamoto core concepts
pages.description: Niamoto runs one pipeline through three config files:

pages.title: CLI quickstart
pages.description: Drive Niamoto from a shell. For the desktop path, read first project.md.

sections.title: Desktop App Tour
sections.description: Use this section when you already have the desktop app installed and want the full UI path from project setup to publication.

pages.title: Desktop App Tour
pages.description: Use this section when you already have the desktop app installed and want the full UI path from project setup to publication.

pages.title: Import
pages.description: Import is where a project becomes usable. Add the source files, let Niamoto detect their roles, review the generated configuration, then load the data into the project workspace.

pages.title: Collections
pages.description: Collections is the reader facing name for the desktop area that manages grouped outputs. Behind the interface, this stage spans config/transform.yml and the collection facing parts of config/export.yml.

headings.title: How this stage fits the full flow

pages.title: Site Builder
pages.description: The Site area turns imported and computed project content into the public portal structure. This is where you shape pages, navigation, and presentation before you move to Publish.

pages.title: Publish
pages.description: Publish is the final desktop stage. Use it to build the generated site, inspect the result, choose a deployment target, and follow the deployment status to the end.

headings.title: 1. Build and inspect the generated site

pages.title: Widget Catalogue and Collection Extras
pages.description: This page is a supporting reference for the Collections area. Use it when the main collections.md page is not enough and you need to focus on widget selection, list pages, or API oriented outputs.

headings.title: Collection extras beyond a single widget

pages.title: Preview Surfaces
pages.description: Preview is not one separate stage of the product. It appears inside three different parts of the desktop workflow.

headings.title: 2. Site preview in the Site Builder

sections.title: Plugin Development
sections.description: Build custom plugins for Niamoto without patching the core runtime.

pages.title: Plugin Development
pages.description: Build custom plugins for Niamoto without patching the core runtime.

pages.title: Plugin System Architecture
pages.description: Niamoto discovers plugins, validates config with Pydantic, then calls the matching runtime hook.

pages.title: Guide de développement de plugins
pages.description: Ce guide explique comment créer des plugins pour la plateforme Niamoto. Les plugins permettent d'étendre les fonctionnalités sans modifier le code principal.

headings.title: Étape 1 : Définir le modèle de paramètres
Étape 2 : Définir le modèle de configuration
Étape 3 : Implémenter la classe du plugin

pages.title: Building Widgets
pages.description: Widgets render HTML for html page exporter. Niamoto validates widget params, calls render(data, params), then wraps the result in the standard widget container.

pages.title: Custom Exporters
pages.description: Exporters turn validated export.yml targets into files, folders, or API payloads. ExporterService passes the whole target config to the plugin, not a loose data/config/output path triple.

headings.title: What Niamoto Passes To The Exporter

pages.title: Database Aggregator Guide
pages.description: Use database aggregator when you need read only SQL inside a transformer entry.

headings.title: Consuming The Result In A Widget

pages.title: Database Aggregator
pages.description: database aggregator is a real transformer plugin. Niamoto ships it in src/niamoto/core/plugins/transformers/aggregation/database aggregator.py.

pages.description: This guide covers exporting biodiversity data in Darwin Core format, the global standard for sharing biological occurrence information. Niamoto's Darwin Core export enables seamless integration with GBIF, iDigBio, and ot

sections.title: Reference
sections.description: This section collects source level and configuration references for Niamoto. Use it when you need the current command surface, plugin contracts, YAML boundaries, database shape, or preview API.

pages.title: Reference
pages.description: This section collects source level and configuration references for Niamoto. Use it when you need the current command surface, plugin contracts, YAML boundaries, database shape, or preview API.

pages.title: CLI Commands Reference
pages.description: The CLI itself is the source of truth for flags and subcommands. This page points to the main command groups and the docs that explain how they fit together.

pages.title: Configuration Guide
pages.description: Niamoto splits configuration by pipeline stage so each file answers one question.

pages.title: Database Schema Reference
pages.description: DuckDB is the default project database at db/niamoto.duckdb. SQLite still works for .db and .sqlite paths, but new desktop projects default to DuckDB.

headings.title: Configuration files that shape the schema
How to inspect a project database

pages.title: Preview API Reference
pages.description: The desktop backend renders HTML previews for widgets and related preview surfaces in the GUI.

pages.title: Transform Plugins Reference
pages.description: Transformer schemas drive several parts of the GUI. The source code and config models remain the source of truth for exact parameters and validation rules.

headings.title: What the GUI reads from plugins
Plugin families commonly surfaced in the GUI

pages.title: Widgets and Transform Workflow
pages.description: Widgets in the GUI combine three layers: data selection, transform logic, and rendering.

pages.title: Core API Reference
pages.description: Niamoto does not maintain a separate handwritten "core API" document. The practical entry points are the autogenerated package reference and the service, plugin, publish, and GUI API modules used by the product.

pages.title: External APIs Reference
pages.description: Niamoto talks to external services in several parts of the product rather than through a single central gateway.

pages.title: API Export Guide
pages.description: Niamoto can publish machine readable outputs alongside the website export. The implementation lives in the exporter layer.

pages.title: Configuration Analysis
pages.description: Use this checklist when reviewing a complex configuration change.

pages.title: YAML Strategies
pages.description: Keep YAML explicit. Reuse only the parts that are genuinely stable.

pages.title: Templates and Hierarchy Patterns
pages.description: Reusable templates help when a block stays stable across several entities or widgets. Hierarchies stay easier to debug when identifiers and parent child relationships remain visible in configuration.

pages.title: Plugin API Reference
pages.description: Niamoto exposes five plugin families across the pipeline:

pages.title: System Overview

pages.title: Plugin System
pages.description: Niamoto uses plugins for the parts of the pipeline that change from one project to another: loading data, computing grouped outputs, rendering widgets, exporting files, and publishing artifacts.

pages.description: Niamoto GUI is the interactive layer used to configure, inspect, and operate a Niamoto project.

pages.description: The preview system renders widgets in the GUI before publication.
